metaTitle: List Randomizer — Free Online List Shuffler & Random Picker
metaDescription: Randomly shuffle a list or pick winners instantly in your browser. Perfect for lotteries, team picks, and decision making. 100% private — no data sent to servers.

howto.description: Type or paste your list items — one per line, or separated by commas.
Shuffle the whole list, or pick a winner from your list.
Click Randomize to instantly shuffle or pick randomly.
Copy the result to your clipboard with one tap.

why: 100% private — your list never leaves your browser
Two modes: full shuffle or single winner pick
Auto-detects list format — no need to choose separators
Remove duplicates option for clean results
Works completely offline after the initial page load
No sign-up, no limits, completely free forever

faq.q: What can I use this tool for?
faq.a: You can use it for lotteries, random team picks, deciding what to eat, shuffling playlists, drawing campaign winners, and any situation where you need a fair random choice.

faq.q: How do I enter my list?
faq.a: Enter one item per line, or separate items with commas. The tool auto-detects the format.
Yes. The tool uses the Fisher-Yates shuffle algorithm with Math.random() for unbiased randomization.
Yes. Enable the Remove Duplicates option before randomizing to ensure each item appears only once.
No. Everything runs in your browser. Your list is never sent to any server or saved anywhere.

technicalSpecs.useCase: List data never leaves your browser
Clean results for lotteries and winners
Shuffles thousands of items in milliseconds

troubleshooting: List not splitting correctly? Ensure your items are separated clearly by a new line or a comma. If you have both, the tool prioritizes new lines.
Wrong winner picked? Every click on 'Randomize' generates a fresh result. If you need a permanent record, make sure to copy the result immediately.
Browser lag with huge lists? Processing lists with over 10,000 items might take a moment. For best performance, keep lists under 5,000 items.
proTips: Use the 'Remove Duplicates' toggle to ensure that each entry has an equal and fair chance without double-counting.
If you're picking a winner for a giveaway, record your screen while clicking 'Randomize' to provide proof of a fair and transparent draw.
Combine this with a case converter if you need to standardize your list (e.g., making all names Uppercase) before shuffling.
